title: HW3 vs HW4 Feature Gap
description: Growing number of features locked to HW4 only leaves HW3 fleet (est. 30% of vehicles) on a diverging software path. Risk of customer backlash and support burden as gap widens.
mitigation: Maintain HW3 feature parity list and publish clear deprecation timeline. Prioritise backports where feasible.

title: Grok AI Beta Stability
description: Grok integration shipped as beta across multiple consecutive releases. Repeated reminder prompts and limited command support indicate backend readiness gaps that could degrade in-car UX.
mitigation: Gate next Grok expansion on P95 latency SLA < 2s. Require beta tag removal sign-off from Liam Torres.

title: Regulatory Risk on Autonomous Features
description: FSD Supervised and upcoming hands-free features face evolving NHTSA and EU type-approval regulations. OTA recall history (5 recalls in dataset) shows exposure.
mitigation: Maintain regulatory watch list. All FSD feature releases require safety-sw and legal sign-off before OTA push.

title: OTA Recall Recurrence
description: Dataset shows 3 OTA recall events (telltale text size
probability: FSD suspension). Pattern suggests insufficient pre-release regression on display-critical and braking code paths.
column10: Mandatory regression suite for any change touching braking
column11: display text or FSD suspension before each release candidate.

title: Rapid Release Cadence Causing Regression
description: 118 versions in 3 years (~39/year) creates high merge pressure. Undocumented Changes (751 of 1782 features) suggest unplanned changes slipping through review.
mitigation: Enforce feature freeze 10 days before release candidate. All undocumented changes require post-hoc ticket before next release.

title: FSD Version Fragmentation
description: Multiple simultaneous FSD versions in field (v12.x and v13.x and v14.x active concurrently). Increases QA surface and complicates rollback decisions.
mitigation: Define max concurrent FSD versions in field as 2. Accelerate deprecation of v12.x branch.

title: Supercharger Routing Dependency on Third-Party Data
description: Supercharger live maps and amenity data rely on real-time feeds. Outages or stale data affect trip planning accuracy and driver confidence.
mitigation: Implement graceful degradation with cached last-known data. Alert Sara Okonkwo on feed latency > 5 min.

title: Child and Pet Safety Feature Reliability
description: Child Left Alone Detection and Dog Mode are safety-critical. Any false-negative in cabin sensing could have life-safety consequences and legal exposure.
mitigation: Priya Nair owns mandatory hardware-in-loop testing on every firmware change touching cabin radar and seat sensors.

title: Bluetooth and Connectivity Regression
description: New Bluetooth menus and Wi-Fi diagnostics shipped across 4 releases show recurring updates suggesting instability. Phone key and ultra-wideband key issues could strand drivers.
mitigation: Add phone key connectivity to smoke test suite. Block release if P99 connection time regresses > 10%.

title: Junior Coverage on Hardware-Critical Paths
description: Noah Kim (junior) is sole owner of lights and suspension firmware. Adaptive headlights and air suspension control failures are safety-adjacent. Bus factor of 1.
mitigation: Pair Noah Kim with Priya Nair for all suspension and headlight code reviews. Document runbooks for on-call escalation.
